name: AIOps Triage System
description: AI-powered alert triage pipeline for NOC/infrastructure teams. Ingests high-volume alerts from heterogeneous sources, classifies severity with Haiku Batch, suppresses false positives via deduplication windows, and routes actionable alerts to on-call channels with Sonnet-generated incident summaries.

defaultStack.rationale: Decouples producers from consumers; handles burst alert traffic without backpressure on downstream classifiers; natural replay for missed events.
Classifies severity, domain, and suppression eligibility at <$0.10/day for 500 alerts/hour. Synchronous Haiku would cost 2× with added latency.
Generates human-readable incident context for P1/P2 alerts only. Hard cap: 20 Sonnet calls per hour to bound cost.
Stateful graph handles the classify → deduplicate → route loop with built-in retry and conditional edges. Airflow is overkill for sub-minute latency requirements.
Alert metadata, deduplication state, and routing log fit in relational storage. No vector store needed unless semantic similarity dedup is in scope.
Standard for on-call routing; deduplicate key maps to alert fingerprint to prevent storm paging.
Track alert volume, classification latency, false positive rate, and Sonnet call count against daily cap.

phaseHints: Phase 1 — Ingestion & normalization: Kafka consumer, per-source schema adapters, SQLite alert store, deduplication fingerprinting
Phase 2 — Tier A classification: Haiku Batch job for severity/domain/suppression labels, confidence scoring, filter pipeline
Phase 3 — Tier B routing: Sonnet incident summary for P1/P2, PagerDuty Events API integration, escalation logic, hard Sonnet cap enforcement
Phase 4 — Observability & tuning: false positive rate dashboard, alert volume trends, Sonnet spend tracker, threshold tuning loop

architectureDecisions.choice: Fingerprint hash (source + alert_name + labels) with 5-minute suppression window
architectureDecisions.rationale: Prevents alert storms from paging on-call N times for the same root cause. Window is configurable per alert class.

architectureDecisions.choice: Only alerts with Haiku severity=P1 or P2 AND confidence>0.8 enter Sonnet
architectureDecisions.rationale: Eliminates ~80% of alert volume from Sonnet spend. Borderline alerts (confidence 0.6–0.8) queue for human review, not auto-escalation.

architectureDecisions.choice: Batch API for Tier A, synchronous for Tier B
architectureDecisions.rationale: Batch saves 50% on Haiku cost for non-latency-sensitive classification. Tier B is synchronous because P1 routing has a sub-60s SLA.

costModelHint: At 500 alerts/hour: Haiku Batch ~$0.08/day for classification. Sonnet capped at 20 calls/hour = max $0.65/day. Total <$0.75/day at full volume. Rate-limit Sonnet calls in the LangGraph node, log overage to stdout.
